generated_oml:
// C1: System Under Study
instance IncubatorCyberPhysicalSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System-based incubator designed for temperature regulation within an insulated container. Operates in thermal control domain with components: insulated container (styrofoam box), heatbed, fan, two internal temperature sensors, one external temperature sensor. Controlled by Raspberry Pi executing closed-loop temperature regulation with dynamic adaptability to varying thermal loads."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment IncubatorEnvironment, CyberPhysicalControlEnvironment
]

instance ThermalControlDomain : DTDFVocab:Domain [
    base:desc "Thermal regulation domain focusing on maintaining predefined setpoint temperature regardless of internal contents, demonstrating dynamic adaptability to thermal loads."
]

instance InsulatedContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box acting as passive thermal barrier to minimize heat loss in the incubator system."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance HeatBed : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element responsible for generating thermal energy to raise internal temperature of the incubator."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance IncubatorFan : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributes heat uniformly within the container to ensure temperature homogeneity in the incubator."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with hardware components to execute closed-loop temperature regulation for the incubator."
    base:isContainedIn CyberPhysicalControlEnvironment
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First internal temperature sensor monitoring temperature inside the styrofoam box for feedback control and safety."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor monitoring temperature inside the styrofoam box for redundant feedback control and safety."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor measuring ambient conditions outside the box to account for environmental influences."
    base:isContainedIn IncubatorEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Two-parameter thermal model representing basic thermal dynamics of the incubator with lower fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, ThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Four-parameter thermal model with improved thermal inertia accuracy for superior predictive fidelity of incubator temperature dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, ThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance ThermalDynamicsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature data collected from sensors representing thermal state of the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, ThermalSimulationEnabler
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical representation of closed-loop control logic for temperature regulation in the incubator."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance SensorCalibrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical and real-time sensor calibration data used to adjust model parameters for accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration algorithms using least-squares optimization or Bayesian inference to update thermal model parameters with real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables ThermalModelRefinementService, DynamicSimulationService
]

instance ThermalSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation engine using differential equation solvers (e.g., SciPy, Simulink) to execute thermal dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService
]

instance ControlAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory controllers and model-predictive control algorithms for setpoint tracking and dynamic adjustment of control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables SafetyOptimizationService, ReconfigurabilityService
]

instance DataProcessingPipelineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Streaming analytics pipeline using Apache Kafka and Flink for real-time feature extraction from sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboards built with Grafana or Plotly Dash for real-time monitoring of temperature profiles and model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdvancedVisualizationService
]

instance RaspberryPiInterfaceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "GPIO/I2C interface on Raspberry Pi for communication with sensors and actuators in the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ClosedLoopControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuously collects time-series temperature data from PT sensors via cyber-physical integration for state estimation and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight, SensorFaultInsight
]

instance DynamicSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes physics-based thermal models to reproduce and predict PT behavior under current/perturbed operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperaturePredictionInsight, WhatIfAnalysisInsight
]

instance ThermalModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Employs calibration algorithms to update CPS and environmental models with real-time sensor data, reducing model-plant mismatch."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ParameterUpdateAction
]

instance SafetyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses supervisory controllers and fault detectors to verify safe thermal operation and trigger corrective actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyComplianceInsight, TemperatureAdjustmentAction
]

instance ReconfigurabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables runtime adaptation to changing conditions by dynamically adjusting control strategies via optimization algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptabilityInsight, ControlParameterUpdateAction
]

instance AdvancedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive dashboards for engineers to visualize system states, model predictions, and operational metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrates sensor-actuator feedback for closed-loop temperature regulation in the incubator system."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlStabilityInsight, ActuatorAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of internal and external temperature readings for current system state assessment."
]

instance SensorFaultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of sensor faults or thermal drift anomalies through statistical process control or machine learning classifiers."
]

instance TemperaturePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasting of temperature deviations based on current thermal dynamics and environmental conditions."
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of potential temperature behavior under perturbed operating conditions (e.g., ambient temperature fluctuations)."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of current model accuracy through comparison with real-time sensor data."
]

instance SafetyComplianceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification that the system operates within safe thermal bounds to prevent overheating or undercooling."
]

instance AdaptabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Evaluation of system's ability to adapt to changing thermal loads or environmental conditions."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of temperature profiles, model predictions, and operational metrics for decision support."
]

instance ControlStabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of closed-loop control stability and performance metrics."
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically updates thermal model parameters based on calibration algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts heatbed power or fan speed to maintain safe temperature bounds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamically adjusts PID gains or other control parameters for optimal performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuatorAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Modifies actuator behavior (e.g., heatbed/fan) based on control algorithm outputs."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AlertGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates alerts for operators when unsafe conditions are detected."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InsulatedContainer, HeatBed, IncubatorFan, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance CyberPhysicalControlEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RaspberryPiController, RaspberryPiInterfaceEnabler, ControlAlgorithmEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via the **Raspberry Pi**, which acts as the cyber-physical interface. The **control commands** are generated by the DT’s algorithms (e.g., supervisory controllers, optimizers) and transmitted to the **heatbed** and **fan** to regulate temperature. The **command types** include **temperature setpoints** and **actuation signals** (e.g., power modulation for the heatbed). The documents do not specify **control message formats**, but they imply a structured communication protocol (e.g., serial/TCP/IP) between the cyber and physical layers. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the DT’s **calibration algorithms** and **monitors** (Document 1, Fig. 1) suggest implicit validation of commands to ensure safety and optimality. **Feedback systems** are present, as the DT continuously monitors temperature sensors to adjust control actions dynamically. **Conflict resolution** is not detailed, but the DT’s **supervisory controllers** likely prioritize safety-critical commands (e.g., emergency shutdowns) over routine adjustments. **Command execution confirmation** is implied via closed-loop monitoring, where the DT verifies the PT’s response against predicted behavior."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator follows a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)-based architecture**, integrating **three core components** as outlined in Fig. 1: **data** (collected via **two internal temperature sensors** and **one external sensor**), **models** (thermal simulation models with **2 or 4 free parameters**, calibrated via **calibration algorithms**), and **algorithms** (e.g., **fault detectors**, **supervisory controllers**, and **state estimators**). The orchestration involves:
  1. **Calibration algorithms** updating the **CPS and environmental models** with real-time sensor data to refine parameter estimates.
  2. **Monitors** (likely implemented in the Raspberry Pi) verifying **safety and optimal performance** of the PT.
  3. **Human or algorithmic intervention** for corrective actions when violations (e.g., temperature deviations) are detected.
  The architecture is **closed-loop**, with the DT continuously synchronizing with the PT via sensor feedback. No explicit mention of **resource management** (e.g., cloud-edge hybrid) or **system-wide coordination patterns** (e.g., microservices, service-oriented) is provided, but the **modularity of models** (two distinct abstractions) suggests **plug-and-play extensibility** for future components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle stages in the provided context include **pre-deployment modeling and simulation phases**, where abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometry or differential equation-based dynamic models for physics) are developed to capture specific aspects of the Physical Twin (PT). These models are later integrated into the DT framework during deployment, where they are continuously updated via **calibration algorithms** to align with real-time sensor data from the PT. The lifecycle also encompasses **operational monitoring**, where the DT employs algorithms (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to assess system performance against predefined safety and optimality criteria. When deviations are detected, the system transitions into **adaptive control phases**, where human or algorithmic interventions modify the PT’s behavior or parameters. The lifecycle governance is implicit in the iterative nature of calibration and monitoring, ensuring the DT remains synchronized with the PT’s evolving state. No explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards for lifecycle management) are mentioned, but the process relies on **real-time data-driven feedback loops** between the PT and DT, as illustrated in Figure 1."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin emphasizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through:
  - **Fidelity requirements**: Models must accurately represent PT behavior under operating conditions. For example, the incubator’s 4-parameter thermal model was chosen over a 2-parameter model due to its superior prediction of thermal inertia, demonstrating higher fidelity.
  - **Validation and verification methods**: Empirical experiments (e.g., comparing model predictions to real-world PT performance) and iterative refinement (e.g., adjusting free parameters based on sensor data).
  - **Uncertainty sources**: Model inaccuracies arise from discrepancies between simulated and real-world conditions (e.g., environmental factors not fully captured in the model).
  - **Error detection and correction**: Calibration algorithms dynamically adjust model parameters (e.g., thermal inertia) using real-time sensor data to minimize errors.
  - **Quality assurance processes**: Continuous monitoring of PT performance via the DT framework, with violations triggering corrective actions (e.g., human or algorithmic interventions to adjust the physical system). The incubator case study’s open-source hardware/software enables reproducible validation of these processes."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT’s technical network infrastructure for the incubator case study relies on a **cyber-physical system (CPS) architecture**, where the **Raspberry Pi** serves as the central node connecting the cyber (software/controller) and physical (sensors/actuators) components. The **communication protocols** are not explicitly specified, but typical CPS implementations use **serial (UART), I2C, or TCP/IP** for sensor-actuator communication, with **Controller Area Network (CAN)** or **Ethernet** for broader system integration. The **network topology** is likely a **star topology** (Raspberry Pi as the hub) or a **hybrid topology** if additional nodes (e.g., cloud servers) are involved. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but the system prioritizes **low-latency, high-reliability** communication for real-time control (e.g., temperature regulation). **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not detailed, though the use of **multiple temperature sensors** (Document 2, Section 2.1) suggests sensor redundancy for fault tolerance. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, but standard CPS practices may include **encryption (TLS), authentication (OAuth), and access control** to protect against cyber threats."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The incubator DT is deployed on a **hybrid hosting infrastructure**, primarily leveraging **on-premise edge computing** (Raspberry Pi) for real-time processing and control, with potential **cloud integration** for advanced analytics or remote monitoring. The **computational requirements** are modest, as the DT runs **two simulation models** (Document 3, Section 1) with **2–4 free parameters**, likely implemented in lightweight frameworks (e.g., Python, MATLAB/Simulink). **Storage requirements** are minimal for the edge deployment but may scale if historical data or logs are archived. **Scalability mechanisms** are not specified, though the modular design of the DT (Document 1) suggests support for **scalable deployment models** (e.g., containerization via Docker or orchestration via Kubernetes) if extended to larger systems. **Availability/reliability assurance** is implied by the DT’s **calibration algorithms** and **monitors** (Document 1, Fig. 1), which ensure continuous operation and fault detection. The **deployment model** is **edge-centric** for the incubator, with optional cloud offloading for non-critical tasks."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The incubator Digital Twin system does not explicitly describe **horizontal integration** with external systems in the provided documents. No details are given regarding:
  - **Data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST APIs) for interfacing with other DTs or enterprise systems.
  - **Interoperability standards** (e.g., FIWARE, Industry 4.0 reference architectures) or dependencies on external systems.
  - **Integration patterns** (e.g., federated DTs, shared data lakes) with other Digital Twins or enterprise-level platforms.
  The system is **self-contained**, focusing solely on the **incubator’s thermal regulation** without references to broader ecosystem integrations. The **publicly available repository** ([GitHub - INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)) may imply potential for **third-party extensions**, but no technical specifications are provided in the documents.
  **Result: Not in Document**"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system integrates **safety-critical monitoring mechanisms** through its **monitoring algorithms**, which continuously assess whether the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its Physical Twin (PT) are operating within safe and optimal parameters. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models with real-time data, ensuring accurate representation of the PT's state. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied in the DT framework, where detected violations (e.g., deviations from safe operating conditions) trigger human or algorithmic interventions to modify the PT's behavior. The DT's **supervisory controllers** and **state estimators** further enhance safety by dynamically adjusting system responses based on real-time data and model predictions. However, the documents do not specify explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), threat mitigation strategies (e.g., intrusion detection), or compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001). The **Incubator case study** lacks detailed references to data governance, privacy protection, or consent management, focusing instead on functional modeling and calibration."
]
